sections.description: Chart.js visualization components, each owning a <canvas> element and the full Chart.js instance lifecycle.
sections.purpose: Provide reactive Chart.js visualizations for dashboard metrics. Each component creates its Chart instance on mount, updates data via watch, and destroys the instance on unmount to prevent memory leaks.
strengths: Each component is self-contained: creates chart on mount, updates on prop change, destroys on unmount
Consistent pattern across all 10 chart components makes them easy to maintain
weaknesses: Chart.js registers all chart types globally (registerables) rather than tree-shaking individual components
Some type assertions needed for Chart.js dataset backgroundColor updates (as unknown as)
sections.test_coverage: No component tests. Charts require canvas/DOM which would need jsdom or happy-dom environment.
sections.functionality:
- TokensOverTime: line chart of token usage by type (input/output/cache read/cache creation) over time
- CostOverTime: line chart of cost by model over time
- LanguagesChart: horizontal bar chart of edit counts by programming language
- ToolUsageDonut: doughnut chart of edit tool usage distribution
- LocOverTime: line chart of lines added/removed over time
- TokensByModel: stacked bar chart of tokens by model and type
- CostByModel: doughnut chart of cost distribution by model
- CostPer1k: bar chart of cost per 1K tokens by model
- TokenTypePie: doughnut chart of token type distribution (input/output/cache)
- ActivityBySession: stacked bar chart of active time by session ID
